questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ductility of bitumen is measured by:
options: The distance in cm to which a briquette can be stretched before breaking
questions.solution: Ductility test: briquette pulled apart at 25°C, 5 cm/min; min 50 cm for paving.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge over a rectangular weir is proportional to:
questions.solution: Q = Cd (2/3) √(2g) B H^(3/2) for rectangular weir.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The permanent adjustment of a level ensures the line of collimation is:
options: Parallel to the axis of the bubble tube
questions.solution: The two-peg test adjusts the line of collimation parallel to the bubble axis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The phenomenon of sudden reduction in compressive strength of a slender column is called:
questions.solution: Slender compression members fail by elastic buckling before yielding.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The final setting time of ordinary Portland cement should not exceed:
questions.solution: IS limits the final setting time of OPC to 600 minutes (10 hours).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fully submerged body, the centre of buoyancy is at the:
questions.solution: Centre of buoyancy = centroid of displaced fluid volume.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In EDM, the principle used is:
options: Measurement of time taken by electromagnetic waves
questions.solution: EDM measures distance via time-of-flight of EM waves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The effective throat thickness of a fillet weld is:
questions.solution: Throat thickness = k×weld size where k = 0.7 for θ = 60-90° (IS 800).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The specific gravity of bitumen is typically:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 2.5 m² with velocity 4 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The whole circle bearing of a line is 310°. Its quadrantal bearing is:
questions.solution: 310° is in NW quadrant: angle from N = 360-310 = 50°W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The design bending strength of a laterally supported beam is:
questions.solution: M_d = β_b Z_p f_y/γ_m0 ≤ 1.2 Z_e f_y/γ_m0 (IS 800 clause 8.2.1.2).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most common timber species used for structural purposes in India is:
questions.solution: Teak is most common for structural/joinery; Sal for heavy construction; Deodar for doors/windows.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the fore bearing of a line is 60°, its back bearing is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the design of a compression member, the effective length for a column with both ends pinned is:
questions.solution: Effective length = L for pinned-pinned columns (IS 800 Table 11).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Particle board is made from:
options: Wood particles bonded with synthetic resin under heat and pressure
questions.solution: Particle board = wood chips/chips + urea-formaldehyde resin, hot pressed.

questions.stem: The velocity head corresponding to a velocity of 2 m/s is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Photo interpretation keys include:
options: Tone, texture, pattern, shape, size, shadow, association
questions.solution: Standard photo-interpretation elements: T, T, P, S, S, S, A.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Prying force in a bolted connection depends on:
options: Bolt diameter, plate thickness, and geometry
questions.solution: Prying force Q = (β/α) × T where β and α depend on geometry.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seasoning of timber reduces:
options: Moisture content to equilibrium moisture content (~12%)
questions.solution: Seasoning = controlled drying to ~12% MC (equilibrium with atmosphere).

questions.stem: The maximum efficiency of a Pelton wheel is about:

questions.stem: If the fore bearing of a line is 120°, its back bearing is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The net effective area for a tension member connected by a single bolt is:
options: Net area of the connected leg only
questions.solution: For single connection: net area = connected leg area (shear lag effect).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The initial setting time of ordinary Portland cement should not be less than:
questions.solution: IS specifies a minimum initial setting time of 30 minutes for OPC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 1.5 m² with velocity 4 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 'offset' in surveying is:
options: A perpendicular or oblique measurement from a survey line to a detail point
questions.solution: Offset = short measurement from chain line to detail (perpendicular preferred).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Block shear failure of a bolted tension member involves:
options: Tension rupture on one plane and shear rupture on another
questions.solution: Block shear tears out a block by tension on one plane and shear on the perpendicular plane.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granite is an example of:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Maxwell-Betti reciprocal theorem states:
options: δ_ij = δ_ji (deflection at i due to unit load at j equals deflection at j due to unit load at i)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A weld used to join two overlapping plates is a:
questions.solution: Fillet welds are used at the edges of overlapping or lapped plates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam and a simply supported beam of equal span carrying the same central load, which is correct?
options: The simply supported beam deflects less.
Deflection depends only on the load, not the supports.
questions.solution: End fixity raises stiffness; the fixed beam's central deflection is one-quarter that of the simply supported beam (PL³/192EI vs PL³/48EI).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The minimum number of bolts in a connection as per IS 800 is:
questions.solution: Min 2 bolts per connection (except for minor/sag rods).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456, the minimum grade of concrete for moderate exposure is:
questions.solution: For moderate exposure, minimum M25 with 0.0035 strain limit.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Comparing a venturimeter and an orifice meter measuring the same discharge, which is correct?
options: The venturimeter has the higher head loss.
The venturimeter has lower head loss and a higher coefficient of discharge.
The orifice meter has lower head loss.
questions.solution: The gradual venturi contraction-expansion minimises separation, giving low permanent head loss and Cd ≈ 0.97–0.99, versus the orifice's sharp, lossy flow.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 20 kPa and friction angle φ = 25°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 80 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 20 kPa, φ = 25°, σ = 80 kPa.
**Working.** $=20+80\times\tan 25^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=57.3$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The flexure (bending) equation is:
questions.solution: The bending equation relates moment, stress and curvature as M/I = σ/y = E/R.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chlorine demand is calculated as:
questions.solution: Chlorine demand = Cl₂ applied - Cl₂ residual after contact time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The overtaking sight distance depends on:
options: Speed of overtaking vehicle, oncoming vehicle, and reaction time
questions.solution: OSD = d₁ + d₂ + d₃ where d₁ = reaction, d₂ = overtaking, d₃ = safety.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The latitude of a line with length 100 m and bearing 60° is:
questions.solution: Lat = L cosθ = 100 cos 60 = 50 m (N). Dep = L sinθ = 86.6 m (E).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: One-way slab is defined when the ratio of longer to shorter span is:
questions.solution: One-way if L_y/L_x > 2 (bending mainly in shorter direction).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Williot-Mohr diagram is used for:
questions.solution: Williot-Mohr = graphical method for truss deflections.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water flows at 0.5 m/s in a 0.5 m diameter pipe of length 100 m, f = 0.02. The friction head loss is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a standard penetration test (SPT), the blow count N is corrected for:
questions.solution: N_corrected = N × C_N × C_DR (overburden + dilatancy corrections).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The characteristic compressive strength of M20 concrete is:
questions.solution: M20 denotes a characteristic cube strength of 20 MPa at 28 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A single-bay single-storey portal frame with fixed feet has a degree of static indeterminacy of:
questions.solution: 6 reactions - 3 eqns = 3. If the frame is symmetric, it can be reduced.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular gate of width 1 m and depth 4 m is submerged vertically with top at water surface. The total hydrostatic force is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries two equal 15 kN point loads placed 2 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 15 kN, a = 2 m, span = 6 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=15$ kN; $M_{max}=15\times2$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=30$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 500 mm, clear cover 30 mm and 25 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A retaining wall of height 4 m retains soil with γ=19 kN/m³, φ=35°. The Rankine active thrust is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An RC beam has effective depth 400 mm, Ast = 1% of bd, fy = 415 MPa, fck = 25 MPa. Its moment capacity (limit state) is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 4 m clay layer (Cc = 0.2, e₀ = 0.6) under an initial effective stress of 100 kPa receives a 80 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.2, H = 4 m, e₀ = 0.6, σ₀ = 100 kPa, Δσ = 80 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.2\times4000}{1+0.6}\log_{10}\tfrac{100+80}{100}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 127.6$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M30 concrete, width 300 mm, effective depth 450 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 30 MPa, b = 300 mm, d = 450 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times30\times300\times450^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 251.5$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has G = 2.7, e = 0.7, S = 0.85. The water content is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a slab simply supported on all four edges with corners held down, the maximum moment occurs at:
questions.solution: Max moment in short-span direction at mid-span for two-way slabs.

questions.stem: An RC beam has effective depth 300 mm, Ast = 1% of bd, fy = 415 MPa, fck = 25 MPa. Its moment capacity (limit state) is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M25 concrete, width 250 mm, effective depth 450 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 25 MPa, b = 250 mm, d = 450 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times25\times250\times450^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 174.7$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An RC beam has effective depth 450 mm, Ast = 1% of bd, fy = 415 MPa, fck = 20 MPa. Its moment capacity (limit state) is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The minimum eccentricity for column design as per IS 456 is:
options: L/500 + D/30 or 20 mm (whichever is more)
questions.solution: e_min = max(L/500 + D/30, 20 mm) where L = unsupported length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water flows through a pipe of diameter 0.3 m at 2.5 m/s. The discharge is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A strip footing of width 2.5 m is founded at depth 1 m. Soil: c=10 kPa, γ=18 kN/m³, Nc=25.1, Nq=12.7, Nγ=9.7. The ultimate bearing capacity (Terzaghi) is:
questions.solution: $q_u = cN_c + \gamma D_f N_q + 0.5\gamma B N_\gamma = 697.9$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of length 2 m carries a 5 kN load at its free end (E = 200 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴). The tip deflection (WL³/3EI) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An end-loaded cantilever has tip deflection $W L^3/3EI$.
**Formula.** $\delta=\tfrac{W L^3}{3 E I}$
**Given.** W = 5 kN, L = 2 m, E = 200 GPa, I = 8×10⁻⁶ m⁴.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{5\times10^3\times2^3}{3\times200\times10^9\times8\times10^{-6}}$ m.
**Answer.** $\delta\approx 8.3$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The degree of static indeterminacy of a fixed beam is:
questions.solution: Fixed beam has 6 reactions - 3 equilibrium eqns = 3 degrees static indeterminacy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The specific speed of a Francis turbine ranges between:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a triaxial test, a specimen fails at deviator stress of 400 kPa under 100 kPa confining pressure. If φ = 30°, the cohesion is:
questions.solution: σ₁ = σ₃ + σ_d = 500 kPa. Using Mohr-Coulomb: σ₁ = σ₃Nφ + 2c√Nφ. Nφ = 3. 500 = 300 + 2c√3. c = 200/(2×1.732) = 57.7 kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a beam, the point of contraflexure is where:
questions.solution: Contraflexure = point where BM changes sign, i.e., BM = 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a central point load of 40 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 40 kN, L = 6 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{40\times6}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=60$ kNm at mid-span.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water flows through a pipe of diameter 0.3 m at 1 m/s. The discharge is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using Manning's formula with n = 0.015, hydraulic radius R = 0.8 m and slope S = 0.001, the mean velocity is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Manning's equation gives mean velocity from roughness, hydraulic radius and bed slope.
**Formula.** $V=\tfrac1n R^{2/3} S^{1/2}$
**Given.** n = 0.015, R = 0.8 m, S = 0.001.
**Working.** $=\tfrac1{0.015}\times0.8^{2/3}\times\sqrt{0.001}$.
**Answer.** $V\approx 1.82$ m/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 2 m wide rectangular channel carries water at 1.5 m depth flowing at 2 m/s. The discharge is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge over a rectangular weir (Cd = 0.62) of length 2 m under a head of 0.5 m is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Discharge over a sharp-crested rectangular weir varies with the 3/2 power of the head.
**Formula.** $Q=\tfrac23 C_d\sqrt{2g}\,L\,H^{3/2}$
**Given.** C_d = 0.62, L = 2 m, H = 0.5 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac23\times0.62\times\sqrt{2\times9.81}\times2\times0.5^{1.5}$.
**Answer.** $Q\approx 1.29$ m³/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The pressure at a depth of 8 m in water (ρ = 1000 kg/m³, g = 9.81 m/s²) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an open channel, the specific energy is minimum at the:
questions.solution: The specific energy curve attains its minimum value at the critical depth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A stepped bar with different cross-sectional areas subjected to axial load has:
questions.solution: Stress = P/A, so area change → stress change.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Euler buckling load for a column with both ends pinned is P_cr = π²EI/L². For a column with one end fixed and the other free, the buckling load is:
questions.solution: Effective length Le = 2L for fixed-free → P_cr = π²EI/(2L)² = π²EI/4L².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 90 MPa and 30 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 90 MPa, σ₃ = 30 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{90-30}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=30$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The unit of strain is:
questions.solution: Strain = change in length/original length = dimensionless.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bar of length L tapers uniformly from diameter d₁ at one end to d₂ at the other. The elongation under axial load P is given by:
questions.solution: δ = ∫(P dx)/(AE) = 4PL/(πEd₁d₂) for linearly tapering circular bar.

questions.stem: An axial load of 150 kN acts on a bar of cross-sectional area 1000 mm². The direct stress is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The detention time of a tank of volume 150 m³ treating a flow of 100 m³/h is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 80 MPa and 20 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 80 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{80-20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=30$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a truss, a zero-force member may be identified when:
options: Two non-collinear members meet at a joint with no external load
questions.solution: If 2 non-collinear members at unloaded joint → both are zero-force members.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Load balancing method in PSC design:
options: Tendon profile is chosen to balance a portion of external load
questions.solution: Load balancing: upward force from curved tendon offsets external loads.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 4 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 10 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 10 kN/m, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times4^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=80$ kNm at the fixed end.
